title: East TN Strategic Plan - 2025 (2)

headings.text: Complete a comprehensive review of strategic goals and KPIs at the end of each quarter, year, and the strategic cycle (Q4 2027).
Assess alignment of annual reports, financial outcomes, and operational decisions with the strategic objectives.
Use membership analytics, event outcomes, and sponsorship performance to inform long-term decisions.
Review the ongoing relevance of the chapter’s mission, vision, and strategic assumptions.
Apply insights to guide the next strategic planning cycle and ensure continuity of successful programs.

headings_flat: Complete a comprehensive review of strategic goals and KPIs at the end of each quarter, year, and the strategic cycle (Q4 2027).
Assess alignment of annual reports, financial outcomes, and operational decisions with the strategic objectives.
Use membership analytics, event outcomes, and sponsorship performance to inform long-term decisions.
Review the ongoing relevance of the chapter’s mission, vision, and strategic assumptions.
Apply insights to guide the next strategic planning cycle and ensure continuity of successful programs.

sections_html.GLOSSARY:
Term

Definition

Formative Evaluation

Formative Evaluation shows what has, or has not been completed to date. This type of evaluation can be undertaken at regular intervals as a form of developmental review.

Frequency/Criteria/Target

How often, and against which criteria, for whom, and with respect to which, if any, goals and policies will the initial evaluation occur? Will there be an initial evaluation only, or a schedule of periodic evaluations?

Goals/Objectives

What goals and objectives, as defined in this strategic plan, are progressing, and how well is the team achieving the goals at the point of evaluation?

Information Usage

What will you do with the information you gather during the formative evaluation?

Move Forward Assessment

What now needs to be done, what has to happen in the future, and what should you desist from doing at this stage?

Alignment

To what extent does your progress so far align with the vision, mission and values espoused in the strategic plan?

Note: Some chapters choose to insert a copy of a scorecard they create and maintain to validate their 3-year vision is being met instead of writing out the above detail. Example below:

Hansen, Jens J. "Woodhill Park Retreat." Two Templates for the Not-for-profit Sector: Strategic Planning for Dummies and an Evaluation Research Template. Woodhill Park Retreat, 2008. Web. 18 Dec. 2015.

Mission

Mission is the series of steps that guide a business to carrying out its vision. To change old and outdated ways of operating, missions must be adopted from the board of directors down to each chapter member.

Objectives

Objectives define whether a mission has been accomplished, usually quantified in the form of key performance indicators (KPIs). To maximize the chances of meeting certain objectives, chapters can adopt the SMART goal attainment strategy.

Priority Scale (H,M,L)

Priority

Meaning

Description

H - High

Must have

Describes an item the chapter must do to be considered a success based on the chapter’s vision for the future

M - Medium

Should have

Represents an item that should be included in annual business plans but could be added in later years or be satisfied in other ways if necessary

L - Low

Nice to have

Describes an item which is considered desirable but not necessary. This will be included if time and resources permit.

Strategies

As objectives guide missions, so too do strategies guide objectives. If the goal of a taxi company was to increase revenue by $10 million annually, a potential strategy may include expanding the service into five new cities by the end of the year.

Strategic Action Programs

Larger programs the organization might create with defined leadership and goals to to support one or more objectives, i.e. Chapter Mentoring Program, Sponsorship Program, Certification Program, Business Analysis Development Day Program, etc. The need for the program is typically defined in the Strategic Plan, but the supporting tactics will be defined by the program team once it’s established within the Strategic Plan period.

Summative Evaluation

Summative Evaluation shows what has, or has not, been achieved insofar as the strategic plan is concerned.

Frequency/Criteria/Target

How often, and against which criteria, for whom, and with respect to which, if any, goals and policies will the final evaluation occur?

Reporting Evaluation

To what extent has your operational plan and the various annual reports prepared addressed the strategic plan?

Data Management

How can the data gathered when completing the summative evaluation be used for informing the next cycle of strategic planning?

Final Assessment and Management

How valid are the tenets the team has operated under for the duration of the strategic plan? Do they still apply, and if not, why should they be revised?

Note: Some chapters choose to insert a copy of a scorecard they create and maintain to validate their 3-year vision is being met instead of writing out the above detail. Example below:

Hansen, Jens J. "Woodhill Park Retreat." Two Templates for the Not-for-profit Sector: Strategic Planning for Dummies and an Evaluation Research Template. Woodhill Park Retreat, 2008. Web. 18 Dec. 2015.

SWOT Analysis

SWOT (strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats) analysis is a framework used to evaluate a company's competitive position and to develop strategic planning. SWOT analysis assesses internal and external factors, as well as current and future potential.

A SWOT analysis is designed to facilitate a realistic, fact-based, data-driven look at the strengths and weaknesses of an organization, initiatives, or within its industry. The organization needs to keep the analysis accurate by avoiding preconceived beliefs or gray areas and instead focusing on real-life contexts. Chapters should use it as a guide and not necessarily as a prescription.

https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/swot.asp

We chose SWOT for this template, but there are other popular methods chapters can use to understand their organizational environment if they choose to. Some include:

  • PESTLE (political, economic, social, technological, legal and environmental) analysis
  • Scenario Planning
  • Critical success factor analysis
  • Porter's Five Forces framework

https://www.nibusinessinfo.co.uk/content/swot-pestle-and-other-models-strategic-analysis#:~:text=These%20include%3A,scenario%20planning

Tactics

Tactics encompass the specific, low-level actions that are taken for strategies to be fulfilled.

Vision

Vision encompasses ideas that summarize where a chapter sees itself in the future. Where will it operate? Which target audience will it serve? How will it position itself against the competition? What does the chapter want to be known for? The answers to these questions should inspire and challenge the chapter to do better without being completely unattainable.

VMOST

The strategic method used to create the Strategic Plan template. The VMOST Analysis is a tool that allows a business to evaluate its core strategies in terms of whether the supporting activities of that strategy are being carried out. The VMOST analysis tries to answer that by looking at five core elements: vision, mission, objectives, strategies, and tactics.

https://brendanbroadhead.wixsite.com/vmost/what-is-vmost

https://fourweekmba.com/vmost-analysis/

sections_html.VISION: The vision for the East Tennessee chapter is to be the trusted hub for business analysis excellence—empowering professionals to connect, grow, and lead change within their careers and communities.
sections_html.MISSION: The mission for the East Tennessee chapter is to make business analysis accessible to all by fostering a collaborative environment where members can develop practical skills, build strong professional connections, and contribute to a dynamic community of practitioners.
sections_html.SWOT ANALYSIS: This section addresses the key strengths, weaknesses, threats and opportunities for the East Tennessee chapter that could or does affect the team’s ability to fulfill its vision and mission.
sections_html.Strengths:
Internal Strengths

Passionate and diverse board

Focus on inclusion and accessibility

Energy and innovation from younger leadership
sections_html.Weaknesses:
Internal Weaknesses

Mitigation

Scattered regional membership

Hybrid/virtual-first programming model

Limited funding base

Build sponsorships aligned with local economic development priorities

Limited member engagement

Targeted outreach, quarterly surveys, visible recognition, and tailored programming

Limited certification among members

Build sponsorships aligned with local economic development priorities

New/inexperienced board

Documented workflows and strong team collaboration
sections_html.Opportunities:
External Opportunities

Workforce development initiatives (city grants, chamber partnerships)

Engaging young professionals (18–37)

Creating alternative learning paths

Access to IIBA global resources and branding
sections_html.Threats:
External Threats

Mitigation

Competing priorities for member time and attention

Focused, engaging, hybrid programming; clear member value messaging

Reliance on volunteer time and capacity

Documented workflows, onboarding, shared tools

Lack of awareness among potential members

Consistent branding, outreach campaigns, partnerships

Focus Areas

KPIs

Strengthen operational infrastructure and communications across the board

Governance & Operations

Membership Engagement

O7

Increase access to BA career development for underrepresented communities

Professional Development

% of programming inclusive of underrepresented groups

O8

Build employer and city alliances to support local talent retention

Partnerships & Outreach

# of exploratory meetings or collaborative activities

sections_html.EVALUATION: The following evaluation methods will show how the Program’s evaluation will be completed.
sections_html.Formative Evaluation:
The section describes the East Tennessee chapter’s evaluation method and/or process it will use to perform a formative evaluation on how the chapter is progressing toward their strategic plan objectives to date.

The East Tennessee chapter will perform it’s formative evaluation in the following manner:

  • Use mid-year surveys and annual member feedback to assess engagement, satisfaction, and value.
  • Evaluate the status of objectives across all focus areas and adjust tactics as needed.
  • Share updates during chapter meetings and newsletters to maintain transparency and accountability.
  • Leverage findings to prioritize high-impact actions and discontinue low-performing initiatives.
sections_html.Summative Evaluation:
The section describes the East Tennessee chapter’s evaluation method and/or process it will use to perform a summative evaluation on how the chapter is progressing toward their strategic plan objectives at the end of the 3 year period.

The East Tennessee chapter will perform its formative evaluation in the following manner:
